differentials.name: renal phosphate wasting (c)  aneurysmal bone cyst
differentials.raw: renal phosphate wasting (c)  aneurysmal bone cyst

differentials.name: swelling + tenderness; limp, pain
differentials.raw: swelling + tenderness; limp, pain (± pathologic fracture)

differentials.name: advanced skeletal + somatic maturation (early) Common location:   rib cage (30%), craniofacial bones [calvarium, mandible] (25%), femoral neck + tibia (25%), pelvis
differentials.raw: advanced skeletal + somatic maturation (early) Common location:   rib cage (30%), craniofacial bones [calvarium, mandible] (25%), femoral neck + tibia (25%), pelvis

differentials.name: Dedifferentiation into osteo- / fibro- /
differentials.raw: Dedifferentiation into osteo- / fibro- / (rarely) chondrosarcoma or malignant fibrous histiocytoma (0.4-1%; more often in polyostotic form)

differentials.name: Pathologic fractures: transformation of woven into lamellar bone may be seen, subperiosteal healing without endosteal healing
differentials.raw: Pathologic fractures: transformation of woven into lamellar bone may be seen, subperiosteal healing without endosteal healing

differentials.name: Paget disease
differentials.raw: Paget disease (older age group, mosaic pattern histologically, radiographically similar to monostotic cranial lesion, outer table involved, usually sparing of facial bones)

differentials.name: Ossifying fibroma
differentials.raw: Ossifying fibroma (narrow zone of transition, displacement of teeth)

differentials.name: HPT
differentials.raw: HPT (polyostotic, no bone expansion, chemical changes, generalized deossification, subperiosteal resorption)

differentials.name: Osteofibrous dysplasia
differentials.raw: Osteofibrous dysplasia (almost exclusively in tibia of children < 10 years + anterior bowing, monostotic, lesion begins in cortex, spontaneous regression)

differentials.name: Neurofibromatosis
differentials.raw: Neurofibromatosis (rarely osseous lesions, vertebral column is primary target, ribbon ribs, cystic intraosseous neurofibroma rare, café-au-lait spots smooth, familial disease)

differentials.name: Simple bone cyst
differentials.raw: Simple bone cyst (more lucent than fibrous dysplasia, not affecting growth plate, straw-colored fluid on aspiration)

differentials.name: Giant cell tumor
differentials.raw: Giant cell tumor (no well-defined sclerotic margin)

differentials.name: Low-grade osteosarcoma
differentials.raw: Low-grade osteosarcoma (invasion of surrounding soft tissues, osteolysis, cortical destruction)

differentials.name: cranial asymmetry, facial deformity; nasal stuffiness
differentials.raw: cranial asymmetry, facial deformity; nasal stuffiness

differentials.name: visual impairment, extraocular muscle palsy Location:  frontal > sphenoid > ethmoid maxilla > zygoma > parietal > occipital > temporal area; orbit [bilateral jaw fullness]
differentials.raw: visual impairment, extraocular muscle palsy Location:  frontal > sphenoid > ethmoid maxilla > zygoma > parietal > occipital > temporal area; orbit (20-39%); hemicranial involvement (DDx: Paget disease is bilateral)

differentials.name: slight upward turning of eyes directed heavenward
differentials.raw: slight upward turning of eyes directed heavenward

differentials.name: Café-au-lait macules (35%) =  few large segmental yellowish to brownish dark tan patches of cutaneous pigmentation with jagged irregular / serrated border = coast of Maine patches (DDx:
differentials.raw: Café-au-lait macules (35%) =  few large segmental yellowish to brownish dark tan patches of cutaneous pigmentation with jagged irregular / serrated border = coast of Maine patches (DDx:

context: May be associated with: (a)  endocrine disorders: ›  precocious puberty in girls ›  hyperthyroidism ›  hyperparathyroidism: renal stones, calcinosis ›  acromegaly ›  diabetes mellitus ›  Cushing syndrome: osteoporosis, acne ›  growth retardation (b)  intramuscular soft-tissue myxoma (rare) =  Mazabraud syndrome: [André Mazabraud (1921-2006), French rheumatologist and pathologist at Curie Institute, Paris] √  typically multiple intramuscular myxomas in vicinity of most severely affected bone √  polyostotic fibrous dysplasia (in 81%); M÷F = 1÷2 Site:   metaphysis is primary site with extension i
